---
title: 'msa_and_tree_generator'
author: 'Jan Huebbers'
date: 'April 2025'
output: html_document
---


```{r setup, include = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, include = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
source('package_loader.R')
# Load ggplot theme
source('Jans_ggplot_theme.R')
```

```{r Directories, warnign = FALSE, include = FALSE}
# Sequence depot
file_path_01 <- "../data/protein_list.xlsx"
Sheet <- "Sheet1"

# Define name for output directory
tree_dir <- "tree_01"

# Plot out
Directory <- file.path("..", "output", tree_dir)

# MSA out
fasta_path <- file.path(Directory, "MSA")

# Create output folders if they do not exist
dir.create(Directory, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create(fasta_path, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
```

```{r Import_sequences, warning = FALSE, include = FALSE}
# Import protein sequences
data <- read_excel(file_path_01, sheet = Sheet)
```

```{r Family_group, warning = FALSE}
# Categorize proteins by family
data <- data %>%
  mutate(sequence = gsub("[^A-Za-z]", "", sequence)) %>% # Remove invalid characters such as '~' from the sequences
  mutate(family = case_when(
    grepl("EXO70|EXOC7|Exoc7", name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "EXO70",
    grepl("EXO84", name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "EXO84",
    grepl("MLO", name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "MLO",
    grepl("CDK", name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "CDK",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  )) %>% # Add protein family column
  relocate(family, .before = name)


# CDK df
CDK_seq <- data %>%
  filter(grepl("CDK", family))
# EXO70 df
EXO70_seq <- data %>%
  filter(grepl("EXO70", family))
# EXO84 df
EXO84_seq <- data %>%
  filter(grepl("EXO84", family))
# MLO df
MLO_seq <- data %>%
  filter(grepl("MLO", family))

write.csv(CDK_seq, "../data/CDK_seq.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(EXO70_seq, "../data/EXO70_seq.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(EXO84_seq, "../data/EXO84_seq.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(MLO_seq, "../data/MLO_seq.csv", row.names = FALSE)

data_msa <- data %>% 
  group_by(family) %>% # Grouping defines hierarchy level of MSA
  nest()
```

```{r Clustal_align, warning = FALSE, include = FALSE}
# Toggle overwriting of existing files
overwrite <- FALSE

# Ensure output directory exists
if (!dir.exists(fasta_path)) dir.create(fasta_path, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Alignment methods to run
methods <- c("ClustalW", "ClustalOmega", "Muscle")

# Helper: map method name to msa() 'method' argument (same strings here)
method_fun <- function(m) switch(m,
  "ClustalW"     = "ClustalW",
  "ClustalOmega" = "ClustalOmega",
  "Muscle"       = "Muscle",
  stop("Invalid method: ", m)
)

for (i in seq_len(nrow(data_msa))) {
  fam <- data_msa$family[[i]]
  df  <- data_msa$data[[i]]

  # Keep only non-empty sequences; uppercase amino acids
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(sequence = toupper(gsub("[^ACDEFGHIKLMNPQRSTVWYBXZJUO]", "", sequence))) %>%
    dplyr::filter(nchar(sequence) > 0)

  if (nrow(df) < 2L) {
    message(sprintf("Skipping '%s': need ≥2 sequences, got %d.", fam, nrow(df)))
    next
  }

  # Safe names
  safe_names <- make.unique(gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9._-]+", "_", df$name), sep = "_")
  Seq <- Biostrings::AAStringSet(df$sequence)
  names(Seq) <- safe_names

  fam_tag <- gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9._-]+", "_", fam)

  for (m in methods) {
    tag <- paste0(fam_tag, "_", m)

    # Paths
    fasta_in  <- file.path(fasta_path, paste0("Seq_", tag, ".fa"))
    rds_out   <- file.path(fasta_path, paste0("Align_data_", tag, ".rds"))
    fasta_out <- file.path(fasta_path, paste0("Seq_align_", tag, ".fa"))

    have_in   <- file.exists(fasta_in)
    have_rds  <- file.exists(rds_out)
    have_out  <- file.exists(fasta_out)

    # If everything exists and overwrite=FALSE, skip entirely
    if (!overwrite && have_in && have_rds && have_out) {
      message(sprintf("Skip %s × %s: all outputs present.", fam, m))
      next
    }

    # Always ensure the unaligned FASTA is written (unless skipping overwrite)
    if (overwrite || !have_in) {
      Biostrings::writeXStringSet(Seq, file = fasta_in)
    }

    # If both aligned outputs already exist and overwrite=FALSE, skip alignment
    if (!overwrite && have_rds && have_out) {
      message(sprintf("Skip alignment for %s × %s: aligned files present.", fam, m))
      next
    }

    # Run alignment
    Align <- tryCatch(
      msa::msa(Seq, method = method_fun(m)),
      error = function(e) {
        message(sprintf("Alignment failed for %s via %s: %s", fam, m, e$message))
        NULL
      }
    )
    if (is.null(Align)) next

    # Save aligned artifacts (respect overwrite)
    if (overwrite || !have_rds)  saveRDS(Align, file = rds_out)

    Seq_align <- as(Align, "AAStringSet")
    if (overwrite || !have_out) Biostrings::writeXStringSet(Seq_align, file = fasta_out)

    # Optional console output
    print(Align, show = "complete")
    message(sprintf("Done: %s (%d seqs) via %s", fam, length(Seq), m))
  }
}
```

```{r Import_alignment_for_tree, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
# ---- Load one alignment for tree building ----
# Choose which family & method to analyze:
family_tag <- "MLO"            # <-- set this
method     <- "ClustalOmega"     # "ClustalW" | "ClustalOmega" | "Muscle"

# Build path consistent with earlier MSA naming
align_rds <- file.path(fasta_path, sprintf("Align_data_%s_%s.rds", family_tag, method))

if (!file.exists(align_rds)) {
  stop(sprintf(
    "Alignment RDS not found: %s\nCheck 'family_tag'/'method' or run the MSA chunk first.",
    align_rds
  ))
}

Align <- readRDS(align_rds)

# User parameters
aa_model <- "JTT"   # e.g., "WAG","JTT","LG","Dayhoff","cpREV","mtmam","VT","rtREV","BLOSUM62", etc.
bts      <- 100      # bootstrap replicates
```

```{r pml_fit_presets_helper, include = FALSE, warning = FALSE}
#' Optimize a pml fit with preset modes
#'
#' @param fit      A phangorn::pml object (e.g., pml(nj_tree, data = phang_align))
#' @param aa_model Protein model string, e.g. "JTT","LG","WAG", etc.
#' @param mode     One of "just-get-it", "light", "heavy"
#' @param trace    0=quiet, 1..3 for verbose optimizer output
#' @param k_light  Discrete gamma categories for 'light' (default 4)
#' @param k_heavy  Discrete gamma categories for 'heavy' (default 4)
#' @param rearr_heavy Rearrangement mode for 'heavy' (default "stochastic")
#' @return Updated pml fit (with optimized tree in $tree)
# helper ---------------------------------------------------------------
optimize_fit_by_mode <- function(
  fit,
  aa_model    = "JTT",
  mode        = c("just-get-it", "light", "heavy"),
  trace       = 1,
  maxit       = 100,   # cap iterations
  k_light     = 4,
  k_heavy     = 4,
  rearr_heavy = "stochastic"
) {
  mode <- match.arg(mode)
  ctrl <- phangorn::pml.control(trace = trace, maxit = maxit)

  if (mode == "just-get-it") {
    # FAST: no topology search, just branch lengths
    fit <- phangorn::optim.pml(
      fit,
      model         = aa_model,
      optInv        = FALSE,
      optGamma      = FALSE,
      optBf         = FALSE,
      optEdge       = TRUE,          # branch lengths only
      rearrangement = "none",        # <- no NNI at all
      control       = ctrl
    )
    return(fit)
  }

  if (mode == "light") {
    fit <- phangorn::optim.pml(
      fit,
      model         = aa_model,
      optInv        = TRUE,
      optGamma      = TRUE,
      k             = k_light,
      optBf         = FALSE,
      optEdge       = TRUE,
      rearrangement = "NNI",
      control       = ctrl
    )
    return(fit)
  }

  # heavy
  fit <- phangorn::optim.pml(
    fit,
    model         = aa_model,
    optInv        = TRUE,
    optGamma      = TRUE,
    k             = k_heavy,
    optBf         = FALSE,
    optEdge       = TRUE,
    rearrangement = rearr_heavy,   # "stochastic"
    control       = ctrl
  )
  fit
}
```

```{r Build_phylo_tree, message = TRUE, warning = FALSE}
# ---- Build ML tree with bootstrap from an 'msa' alignment ----
# Ensure output directory exists
if (!dir.exists(Directory)) dir.create(Directory, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Convert alignment -> character matrix -> phyDat
# Primary route
aligned_char <- tryCatch({
  as.matrix(Align@unmasked)  # msa::MsaAAMultipleAlignment -> DNAStringSet/AAStringSet
}, error = function(e) {
  # Fallback route via AAStringSet
  aa <- as(Align, "AAStringSet")
  as.matrix(aa)
})

if (length(aligned_char) == 0) stop("The alignment appears to be empty or not read correctly.")

# phangorn data with site-pattern compression for speed
phang_align <- phangorn::phyDat(aligned_char, type = "AA")

# quick stats
n_seq  <- length(phang_align)           # number of sequences
n_sites <- attr(phang_align, "nr")       # number of site patterns
message(sprintf("Data: %d sequences, %d site patterns.", n_seq, n_sites))

# Distance -> NJ starting tree
dist_matrix <- phangorn::dist.ml(phang_align, model = aa_model)
nj_tree     <- phangorn::NJ(dist_matrix)
nj_tree     <- ape::unroot(nj_tree)

# ML fit and optimization
fit0 <- phangorn::pml(nj_tree, data = phang_align)
## pick one:
fit_just  <- optimize_fit_by_mode(fit0, aa_model = aa_model, mode = "just-get-it")
#fit_light <- optimize_fit_by_mode(fit0, aa_model = aa_model, mode = "light")
#fit_heavy <- optimize_fit_by_mode(fit0, aa_model = aa_model, mode = "heavy")

# Bootstrap support
set.seed(1710)
bs <- phangorn::bootstrap.pml(
  fit_just,
  bs        = bts,
  optNni    = FALSE,# optNni = TRUE → For each bootstrap replicate, phangorn resamples columns, then searches topology with NNI (plus branch-length optimization) to maximize likelihood on that resampled alignment.
  multicore = FALSE,
  control   = phangorn::pml.control(trace = 0))

# Extract ML tree
ml_tree <- fit_just$tree

# Save artifacts (RDS + Newick) with informative names
base_tag <- sprintf("%s_%s_%s_%s", family_tag, method, aa_model, bts)
saveRDS(ml_tree, file = file.path(Directory, sprintf("ML_Tree_%s.rds", base_tag)))
saveRDS(bs,      file = file.path(Directory, sprintf("BS_%s.rds", base_tag)))
ape::write.tree(ml_tree, file = file.path(Directory, sprintf("ML_Tree_%s.newick", base_tag)))
message(sprintf("Saved ML tree and bootstrap: %s", base_tag))
```

```{r TreeValidation, fig.height=40/2.54, fig.width=40/2.54, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, include=TRUE}
# ---- Load ML tree and bootstrap replicates ----
tree_path <- file.path(Directory, sprintf("ML_Tree_%s.rds", base_tag))
bs_path   <- file.path(Directory, sprintf("BS_%s.rds", base_tag))

if (!file.exists(tree_path)) stop("Missing ML tree: ", tree_path)
if (!file.exists(bs_path))   stop("Missing bootstrap file: ", bs_path)

ml_tree <- readRDS(tree_path)   # class 'phylo'
bs      <- readRDS(bs_path)     # list of bootstrap trees

# ---- (Optional) midpoint root & ladderize for nicer layout ----
# Comment these two lines if you prefer the original topology orientation
ml_tree <- ape::ladderize(ml_tree, right = TRUE)

# ---- Quick base plotting with supports (>= 50) ----
op <- par(no.readonly = TRUE); on.exit(par(op))
par(mar = c(1, 1, 1, 1))
phangorn::plotBS(ml_tree, bs, p = 50, type = "phylogram", cex = 0.5)  # shrink tip labels a bit

# ---- Build a support-annotated tree object WITHOUT plotting ----
# type = "none" returns the tree with node.label = bootstrap values
tree_with_bs <- phangorn::plotBS(ml_tree, bs, p = 0, type = "none")  # keep all supports on object
# Unroot for downstream functions that expect unrooted
ml_tree_input <- ape::unroot(tree_with_bs)

# ---- Your custom ggplot-based rendering (guard if file missing) ----
tmpl <- "tree_templates/Phylogram_witho_meta.R"
if (!file.exists(tmpl)) stop("Missing template script: ", tmpl)
source(tmpl)

# Example: You can pass support threshold to your function if supported; otherwise it will use node.label
plot_phylo_test <- Phylo_test(tree = ml_tree_input, LayO = "daylight")
print(plot_phylo_test)

# ---- Save SVG (40×40 cm is big; adjust if needed) ----
out_svg <- file.path(Directory, sprintf("Phylo_test_daylight_%s.svg", base_tag))
ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename  = out_svg,
  plot      = plot_phylo_test,
  width     = 40, height = 40, units = "cm",
  limitsize = FALSE
)

# ---- Save the annotated tree for later reuse ----
saveRDS(ml_tree_input, file = file.path(Directory, sprintf("Tree_%s.rds", base_tag)))
message("Saved: ", out_svg)
```

```{r TreeDataSetup, warning = FALSE, include = FALSE}
# Load optimized ML tree with bootstrap values
tree_path <- file.path(Directory, sprintf("Tree_%s.rds", base_tag))
ml_tree_input <-  readRDS(tree_path)

# Convert tree with bootstrap values into a tibble and prepare for MRCA extraction and color mapping
ml_tibble_w_metadata <- ml_tree_input %>%
                        as.tibble() %>%
                        mutate(
                          bootstrap = ifelse(!is.na(as.numeric(label)), as.numeric(label), NA_real_),  # Extract bootstrap values
                          label = ifelse(is.na(as.numeric(label)), label, NA_character_)                    # Keep only non-numeric labels
                        ) %>%
                        left_join(data, by = c("label" = "name")) %>%  # Merge tree data with external data
                        select(c(parent, node, branch.length, label, bootstrap, clade, species, chain_1)) # Keep relevant columns
```

```{r getMRCA_chunk, warning = FALSE, include = FALSE}
# Access MRCA_get function
source("MRCA_get.r")

# Compute MRCA nodes and descendant nodes for each clade
ml_tibble_MRCA <- ml_tibble_w_metadata %>%
                  group_by(clade) %>%         # Group by 'clade'
                  nest() %>%                  # Nest the data for each clade
                  mutate(
                    # Calculate MRCA for each clade group
                    mrca_node = purrr::map_int(data, get_clade_mrca, tree = ml_tree_input)  # <- int column
                  ) %>%
                  unnest(data) %>%            # Unnest the nested clade data
                  relocate(clade, .before = mrca_node) # Move 'clade' column before 'mrca_node'
```

```{r Color_tree, warning = FALSE, error = FALSE}
# --- Build node labels for coloring (one per row) ---
node_count <- max(ml_tibble_MRCA$node, na.rm = TRUE)

ml_tibble_color <- ml_tibble_MRCA %>%
                    arrange(node) %>%
                    mutate(Node_color = paste0("Node_", node))  # one-to-one with each row

# --- What things can be colored? nodes + clades + species ---
clades_present   <- sort(unique(na.omit(ml_tibble_color$clade)))
species_present  <- sort(unique(na.omit(ml_tibble_color$species)))
node_keys        <- paste0("Node_", seq_len(node_count))

color_ids <- c(node_keys, clades_present, species_present)

# --- Base color map (default grey) ---
base_color <- "#9C9E9F"
Colors <- setNames(rep(base_color, length(color_ids)), color_ids)

# --- Clade gradient (auto-sized to detected clades) ---
clade_colors_start <- "#0098A1"
clade_colors_end   <- "#F6A800"
if (length(clades_present) > 0) {
  clade_gradient <- colorRampPalette(c(clade_colors_start, clade_colors_end))
  clade_colors   <- setNames(clade_gradient(length(clades_present)), clades_present)
  Colors[names(clade_colors)] <- clade_colors
}


# --- Color MRCA nodes in black ---
mrca_nodes_color <- ml_tibble_color %>%
  filter(!is.na(mrca_node)) %>%
  distinct(mrca_node) %>%
  pull(mrca_node)

if (length(mrca_nodes_color) > 0) {
  mrca_overrides <- setNames(rep("#000000", length(mrca_nodes_color)),
                             paste0("Node_", mrca_nodes_color))
  # only apply for keys that exist
  mrca_overrides <- mrca_overrides[names(mrca_overrides) %in% names(Colors)]
  Colors[names(mrca_overrides)] <- mrca_overrides
}

# --- Optional: manual species overrides (example) ---
manual_overrides <- c(
  "Arabidopsis thaliana"       = "#2C2D2D",
  "Chara braunii"              = "#858788",
  "Marchantia polymorpha"      = "#595A5A",
  "Mesotaenium endlicherianum" = "#6F7071",
  "Hordeum vulgare"            = "#000000"
)
manual_overrides <- manual_overrides[names(manual_overrides) %in% names(Colors)]
Colors[names(manual_overrides)] <- manual_overrides

# Inspect
print(Colors)


### How to use `Colors` when plotting
# If your plotting code maps **a column that contains `Node_*`, clade, or species names** to color, add:
# + scale_color_manual(values = Colors, na.value = base_color)
```

```{r TreeDataFinal, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# --- Final meta tibble for plotting -----------------------------------------

# Ensure MRCA node is integer and add a stable key matching your Colors map
ml_tree_meta <- ml_tibble_color %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mrca_node = as.integer(mrca_node),
    node_key  = paste0("Node_", node)      # one-to-one with node id
  )

# Collect unique MRCA nodes, omitting NA
mrca_collect <- stats::na.omit(unique(ml_tibble_MRCA$mrca_node))
print(mrca_collect)

# Unroot only if needed (MRCA is topology-based; unrooting won't change it)
if (ape::is.rooted(ml_tree_input)) {
  ml_tree_input <- ape::unroot(ml_tree_input)
}

# Table of MRCA nodes per clade (for clade ribbons/labels etc.)
mrca_nodes <- ml_tree_meta %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(mrca_node), !is.na(clade)) %>%
  dplyr::distinct(mrca_node, clade) %>%
  dplyr::rename(node = mrca_node, label = clade) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(node_key = paste0("Node_", node))

# (Optional) attach color from your Colors vector if you want it baked in:
if (exists("Colors")) {
  # Colors is a named vector with names like "Node_<id>", clade names, species names
  col_lookup <- tibble::tibble(key = names(Colors), color = unname(Colors))

  # node colors (using node_key) and clade colors (using label)
  mrca_nodes <- mrca_nodes %>%
    dplyr::left_join(col_lookup, by = c("node_key" = "key")) %>%  # node-based color
    dplyr::rename(node_color = color) %>%
    dplyr::left_join(col_lookup, by = c("label" = "key")) %>%     # clade-based color
    dplyr::rename(clade_color = color)
}

# (Optional) tip-level meta (handy for tip coloring/labels)
tip_meta <- ml_tree_meta %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(label)) %>%
  dplyr::select(node, node_key, label, species, clade, chain_1, bootstrap)

# Objects you can pass to your visualizer:
# - ml_tree_input: phylo (unrooted if it was rooted)
# - ml_tree_meta: per-node tibble (has node_key)
# - mrca_nodes: clade MRCA table (node, label, node_key, optional colors)
# - Colors: named vector for scale_color_manual(values = Colors)
# - labels_to_show: named vector for protein names to be shown as tip labels
labels_to_show <- c("AtMLO1", "AtMLO2", "AtMLO3", "AtMLO4", "AtMLO5", "AtMLO6", "AtMLO7", "AtMLO8", "AtMLO9", "AtMLO10", "AtMLO11", "AtMLO12", "AtMLO13", "AtMLO14", "AtMLO15", "CbMLO1", "CbMLO2", "CbMLO3", "CbMLO4", "CbMLO5", "CbMLO6", "MeMLO1", "MpMLO1", "MpMLO2", "MpMLO3", "MpMLO4", "HvMlo1")
```

```{r Plot_daylight, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=TRUE, fig.height=30/2.54, fig.width=40/2.54}
# --- Load function for tree visualization
tmpl <- file.path("tree_templates", "Phylogram_daylight_default.R")
if (!file.exists(tmpl)) stop("Template not found: ", tmpl)
source(tmpl)

# --- Build plot (padding + no clipping so labels can extend)
plot_phylo_day <- phylo_daylight(
  tree = ml_tree_input,
  metadata = ml_tree_meta,
  clade_nodes = mrca_nodes,
  color_mapping = Colors
) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.10)) + # Add 20% padding on both sides
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = 0.10)) # Add 20% padding on both sides
  # coord_cartesian(clip = "off") +
  # theme(plot.margin = margin(10, 10, 10, 10))
 
# --- Display
plot_phylo_day

# --- Save
ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(Directory, paste0("Phylo_daylight_", method, "_", aa_model, "_", bts, ".svg")),
  plot = plot_phylo_day,
  width = 40, height = 30, units = "cm",
  limitsize = FALSE
)
```

```{r Clado_circ function, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
Clado_circ <- function(tree, metadata, color_mapping, clade_nodes,
                       show_legend = FALSE, use_equal_edges = TRUE) {

  # Base tree
  p <- ggtree(
    tree,
    layout = "circular",
    color  = "#646567",
    size   = 0.5,
    branch.length = if (use_equal_edges) "none" else "branch.length"
  )

  # Attach node/tip metadata BEFORE adding layers that use it
  p <- p %<+% metadata

  # Tip points (squares), filled by Node_color; angle is computed by ggtree for circular layouts
  p <- p +
    ggtree::geom_tippoint(
      data = function(x) dplyr::filter(x, label %in% labels_to_show),
      ggplot2::aes(fill = species), angle = 0, color = "#000000", shape = 21, size = 2, alpha = 0.9
      ) +

    # Tip labels as small boxed labels, filled by clade (optional; remove 'fill = clade' if not desired)
    geom_tiplab(
      aes(fill = clade, angle = angle),
      align = FALSE,
      color = "#000000",
      hjust = -0.2,
      size = 1.2,
      offset = 0.0,
      geom = "label",
      label.padding = grid::unit(0.25, "lines"),
      label.r       = grid::unit(0.0,   "lines"),
      label.size    = 0.2
    )

    # Internal nodes (circles), filled by Node_color
    # geom_nodepoint(aes(fill = Node_color), shape = 21, size = 1)

  # Scales: same palette for color and fill so clade labels & MRCA bars match, and Node_* keys resolve
  p <- p +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_mapping) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_mapping)

  # Optional: hide legends
  if (!show_legend) {
    p <- p + guides(color = "none", fill = "none", size = "none", shape = "none")
  }

  # Clade labels from MRCA nodes, colored by the clade label
  if (!is.null(clade_nodes) && nrow(clade_nodes) > 0) {
    p <- p +
      geom_cladelab(
        data = clade_nodes,
        mapping = aes(node = node, label = label, color = label),
        align        = TRUE,
        hjust        = "center",
        offset       = 3.7,
        offset.text  = 0.3,
        barsize      = 0.8,
        fontsize     = 4,
        fontface     = "bold",
        angle        = "auto",
        horizontal   = FALSE,
        parse        = FALSE,
        textcolor    = "#000000"
      )
  }
  # p <- ggtree::rotate(p, 395) # does not rotate labels or coloring
  p
}
```

```{r Plot_circ, echo=FALSE, fig.height = 40.0/2.54, fig.width = 40.0/2.54, warning=FALSE, include=TRUE}
# Create Tree
plot_clado_circ <- Clado_circ(
  tree          = ml_tree_input,
  metadata      = ml_tree_meta,
  color_mapping = Colors,
  clade_nodes   = mrca_nodes,
  show_legend   = FALSE,
  use_equal_edges = TRUE
)

plot_clado_circ

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(Directory, paste0("Clado_circ_", method, "_", aa_model, "_", bts, ".svg")),
  plot     = plot_clado_circ,
  width    = 40, height = 40, units = "cm",
  limitsize = FALSE
)
```
